decisions.decision: Keep this leaf at the existing `frontend/src/__tests__/MarketList.test.tsx` seam and prove the route-visible contract there, instead of adding lower-level `useMarkets(...)` or query-key assertions.
alternatives_rejected: Add a new hook-level normalization test for `useMarkets(...)` as part of this leaf.
Split the work across route tests and separate query-key assertions even though the route already exposes the request shape and rendered output.

decisions.decision: Replace the shared `mockResolvedValue(...)` setup with a request-aware `listMarkets` mock that branches on the received filter argument and returns contract-consistent fixtures for the default, active, and resolved paths.
alternatives_rejected: Keep one shared fixture for every request and rely only on `toHaveBeenCalledWith(...)` assertions.
Use `mockResolvedValueOnce(...)` call-order sequencing instead of tying returned rows to the actual request argument.

decisions.decision: Make every `MarketListResponse` fixture internally consistent with the backend-normalized contract, including `meta.filters.include_resolved: false` for default and active collections and `true` for the resolved collection.
alternatives_rejected: Preserve lightweight but contradictory fixtures that include resolved rows while claiming `include_resolved: false`.
Ignore `meta.filters` because the route does not currently read it.

decisions.decision: Freeze the two halves of the route contract in paired request-and-render assertions: the blank dropdown state must call `listMarkets(undefined)` and hide resolved rows, while explicit `active` and `resolved` selections must call `{ status: ... }` and render the matching collection without asserting a route-level `{ includeResolved: true }` input.
alternatives_rejected: Assert only request arguments and skip proving which rows are rendered for each selection.
Expect the route to pass `{ includeResolved: true }` for the resolved branch even though that normalization belongs below the route.

steps.task: Refactor `frontend/src/__tests__/MarketList.test.tsx` fixture setup so the suite has distinct default, active, resolved, and empty responses whose `markets`, `count`, and `meta.filters` agree with one another and with the backend's effective filter contract.

Replace the current `beforeEach` mock configuration with a request-aware `listMarkets` mock implementation that returns the default response for `undefined`, the active response for `{ status: "active" }`, the resolved response for `{ status: "resolved" }`, and a targeted empty response only in the empty-state test.

Rewrite the status-filter assertions so the initial render proves `listMarkets(undefined)` and the absence of resolved rows in the all-status view, then verify that selecting `active` and `resolved` triggers the expected `{ status: ... }` calls and swaps the rendered collection to the matching active-only or resolved-only markets.

Run the focused Vitest coverage for `frontend/src/__tests__/MarketList.test.tsx`, adjust any remaining smoke or empty-state assertions to use the new request-aware helpers, and review the diff to confirm the leaf stayed scoped to this route test.
